story: On the long drive to Grandma’s, Emily and Mark police an invisible line down the backseat. Emily’s stuffed cat, Mr. Whiskers, has a loose button eye. Mark offers to fix it; he’s sewn a Scout badge and says he’ll use black thread and a needle he’s sure Grandma can help him find. Their parents ride up front; fields slide by. On Grandma’s kitchen table, a note waits: “Borrowed the mending tin—back later. —Aunt June.” The car hums on; Mom dozes, Dad tucks a strand of her hair behind her ear. Emily shares Oreos, setting one on the neutral line like a treaty. A billboard boasts the best pie in three counties as the highway carries them toward Grandma’s.
implausible_knowledge: On Grandma’s kitchen table, a note waits: “Borrowed the mending tin—back later. —Aunt June.”
question: On the way to Grandma’s, what is Mark most likely to suggest doing about fixing Mr. Whiskers?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask their dad to stop at a store to buy a small sewing kit on the way.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep driving to Grandma’s and plan to ask her for a needle when they arrive.
